title: Quantum resolution of the Schwarzschild singularity
authors: Vishnulal Cheriyodathillathu, Tanmay Patil, Saurya Das, Soumen Basak
abstract: We revisit the Schwarzschild singularity in a semiclassical setting where the background geometry is classical and quantum effects enter through Bohmian (quantal) trajectories associated with a Klein Gordon wave packet. Using the Madelung-Bohm decomposition of the Klein Gordon wavefunction, we show that the quantum-modified motion is equivalent to geodesic motion in an effective metric conformally related to Schwarzschild, with a conformal factor fixed by the wavefunction amplitude. Solving the wavefunction equation near $r\to 0$ determines this factor and yields finite curvature invariants, in suitable coordinates the interior extends smoothly and the effective spacetime is geodesically complete. This suggests that quantum dynamics on a fixed classical background can regularize the Schwarzschild singularity without a full theory of quantum gravity.
summary: This paper proposes that the Schwarzschild singularity can be regularized without a full theory of quantum gravity by considering the effective geometry seen by quantum probes. By using Bohmian mechanics, the authors demonstrate that the singularity is replaced by an asymptotic boundary with finite curvature. This suggests that quantum dynamics on a classical background can fundamentally alter the causal structure of black holes.
main_problem: The paper investigates whether the Schwarzschild singularity at r=0 can be resolved using a semiclassical approach where quantum effects are modeled via Bohmian trajectories.
main_result: The authors show that the effective geometry experienced by a quantum probe has finite curvature invariants and is geodesically complete, effectively rendering the classical singularity physically inaccessible.
method: The study uses the Madelung-Bohm decomposition of the Klein-Gordon wavefunction to map non-geodesic trajectories onto geodesic motion in a conformally related effective metric.
model_or_system: A massive scalar field (Klein-Gordon field) propagating in a fixed, classical Schwarzschild spacetime background.
key_observables: Kretschmann scalar (curvature invariant), conformal proper time, and affine parameter.
important_parameters: The limit r approaching 0, the conformal factor scaling exponent n, and the coupling constants xi and eta.
main_assumptions: The background Schwarzschild geometry is assumed to be fixed and classical, neglecting the back-reaction of the quantum probe on the spacetime.
paper_structure: The paper begins by defining the semiclassical framework and Bohmian trajectories, moves to the derivation of the effective conformal metric, performs an asymptotic analysis of curvature invariants near the origin, and concludes with an evaluation of geodesic completeness.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
